revision.comment: Bot: mark 3 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24155476638|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:仿製鏡]]
{{nihongo|{{ill|Bōseikyō|ja|Bōseikyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōseikyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551120}}|仿製鏡|qq=}} also known as "倡製鏡" {{ill|imitation mirrors|ja|倣製鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imitation mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仿製鏡|1=Bōseikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q130529515}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=國史大轍典|publisher=吉川廣文館|chapter=倡製鏡|author={{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Otsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433969}}|qq=}}</ref> refers to {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|qid=Q35197|lt=bronze mirrors|1=Mirror}} crafted in regions surrounding {{ill|Mainland China|en|Mainland China|qid=Q19188|lt=continental China|1=Mainland China}}, modeled after original Han-style mirrors. These types of mirrors are also distributed in regions like the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}, {{ill|Central Asia|en|Central Asia|qid=Q27275|lt=Central Asia|1=Central Asia}}, and {{ill|Vietnam|en|Vietnam|qid=Q881|lt=Vietnam|1=Vietnam}}, but the majority are produced in the {{ill|Japanese archipelago|en|Japanese archipelago|qid=Q841337|lt=Japanese archipelago|1=Japanese archipelago}}.<ref name=":1">{{Kotobank|encyclopedia={{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|qid=Q2660112|lt=Nipponica|1=Encyclopedia Nipponica}}|word=倡製鏡|qq=}}</ref> == Overview == {{ill|Bōseikyō|ja|倡製鏡|1=Bōseikyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōseikyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōseikyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a term used for mirrors created in the {{ill|Japanese archipelago|en|Japanese archipelago|qid=Q841337|lt=Japanese archipelago|1=Japanese archipelago}} from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, distinct from the {{ill|Wa mirrors|ja|和鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wa mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551119}} made during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} and beyond, which were based on Tang and Song mirrors.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|title=Revised Edition: World Encyclopedia|year=2007|publisher=Heibonsha|author={{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中璿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|chapter=Bōseikyō|qq=}}</ref> Mirrors imported directly from China are called {{ill|Hakuzaikyō|ja|般載鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuzaikyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contrasting with Bōseikyō. Unlike Han-style mirrors, Bōseikyō often have deteriorated representations of divine beasts or inscriptions that are merely decorative patterns with no meaning.<ref name=":0" /> The quality of bronze and casting precision is also lower than Han-style mirrors.<ref name=":0" /> In terms of {{ill|bronze artifacts|ja|青銅器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bronze artifacts|1=bronze object|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4390114}}, Bōseikyō contains less {{ill|tin|ja|錫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スズ|1=Tin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1096}} and exhibits lower polishing accuracy, resulting in poor quality as mirrors.<ref name=":2" /> However, features like {{ill|Rei-kyō|en|Rei-kyō|qid=Q85884145|lt=suzukagami|1=Rei-kyō}} (mirrors with bells) and other practical yet unconventional designs suggest their primary use as sacred objects rather than functional mirrors in ancient {{ill|Wa|ja|倭國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name=":1" /> The earliest Bōseikyō crafted in the Yayoi period were based on mirrors from the {{ill|Han dynasty|ja|前漢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Han|qid=Q1072949}}, further copied from {{ill|Korean Peninsula mirrors|ja|朝鮮鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name=":1" /> Known as Yayoi Bōseikyō, small Bōseikyō, or archaic Bōseikyō, they were mainly produced in northern {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|qid=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}}, with diameters around 7 cm, often featuring faint patterns and being crudely made.<ref name=":2" /> However, Bōseikyō production became more active during the Kofun period.<ref name=":0" /> The relationship between Bōseikyō used as burial accessories in {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} and earlier Yayoi mirrors is unclear. Some mirrors from this period, such as {{ill|Inner Flower Pattern Mirrors|ja|內行花文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Flower Pattern Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Square-God Mirrors|ja|方格規矩四神鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-God Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, closely replicate Chinese designs but may have reversed motifs due to direct impression molding from imported mirrors. This process led to the production of {{ill|Fumigaeshi Mirrors|ja|踏返鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumigaeshi Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name=":2" /> Later, Bōseikyō with designs distinct from Chinese prototypes emerged. Examples include: * {{ill|Beast-Shaped Mirrors|ja|獣形鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beast-Shaped Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose imagery does not match any divine beasts from Chinese tradition. * {{ill|Twisting Pattern Mirrors|ja|捨文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twisting Pattern Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which extract and repeat elements like feathers from divine beasts. * {{ill|Nipple Pattern Mirrors|ja|乳文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nipple Pattern Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Beaded Pattern Mirrors|ja|珠文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beaded Pattern Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, featuring large projections (nipples) and small beaded designs, respectively. * {{ill|Taryū Mirrors|ja|鼗龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taryū Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, not based on recognized divine beasts, suggesting artisans devised these patterns independently. * {{ill|Straight Arc Pattern Mirrors|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Pattern Mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, {{ill|House Pattern Mirrors|ja|屋文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House Pattern Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hunting Pattern Mirrors|ja|猎午文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hunting Pattern Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which exhibit uniquely Japanese iconography. Mirrors with bells along their perimeters, like {{ill|Rei-kyō|en|Rei-kyō|qid=Q85884145|lt=Suzukagami|1=Rei-kyō}}, deviate significantly from traditional Chinese designs.<ref name=":2" /> {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中璿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1977) argued that Bōseikyō featuring unique Japanese motifs were "not mere imitations," leading to a preference for the term {{ill|Wa-made Mirrors|ja|倭製鏡|1=Bōseikyō|lt=Wa-produced mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wa-made Mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōseikyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仿製鏡}} to describe mirrors produced in Japan.<ref>{{Cite book|author={{ill|Jun'ichirō Tsujita|ja|輝田淳一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun'ichirō Tsujita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|title=Ancient History of Mirrors|year=2019|publisher={{ill|Kadokawa Corporation|en|Kadokawa Corporation|qid=Q17224985|lt=KADOKAWA|1=Kadokawa Corporation}}|series=Kadokawa Selection|isbn=978-4-04-703663-5|pp=15-17|qq=}}</ref> == References == <references /> == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author={{ill|Jun'ichirō Tsujita|ja|輝田淳一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun'ichirō Tsujita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|title=Ancient History of Mirrors|year=2019|publisher={{ill|Kadokawa Corporation|en|Kadokawa Corporation|qid=Q17224985|lt=KADOKAWA|1=Kadokawa Corporation}}|series=Kadokawa Selection|isbn=978-4-04-703663-5|qq=}} {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} ==See Also== Straight Arc Mirror '''Chokkomon Mirror''' (直弧文鏡) is a type of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} (Bōseikyō) produced during the [[Kofun period]] in the [[Japanese archipelago]]. ... 3 KB (342 words) - 06:58, 21 2025 Sea-beast grape mirror ...increased their prominence. Japan also produced {{ill|imitation mirrors|ja|Bōseikyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imitation mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551120}} domestically. In China, these mirrors are more commonly referred to as '' ... 6 KB (749 words) - 05:39, 20 2025 Shibagahara Kofun Group * Four-animal bronze mirror (Bōseikyō) – Diameter: 12 cm ... 8 KB (966 words) - 06:46, 21 2025 * [[Straight Arc Mirror]] '''Chokkomon Mirror'''(直弧文鏡) is a type of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}('''Bōseikyō''') produced during the [[Kofun period]]in the [[Japanese archipelago]]. ... 3 KB (342 words) - 06:58, 21 2025 * [[Sea-beast grape mirror]] ...increased their prominence. Japan also produced {{ill|imitation mirrors|ja|'''Bōseikyō'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imitation mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551121}} domestically. In China, these mirrors are more commonly referred to as ''... 6 KB (749 words) - 05:39, 20 2025 * [[Shibagahara Kofun]]** Four-animal bronze mirror ('''Bōseikyō''') – Diameter: 12 cm ... 8 KB (966 words) - 06:46, 21
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
